primary_accounting_issue: Eligibility for hedge accounting of foreign exchange risk component of intragroup dividend receivable in consolidated financial statements

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern whether intragroup monetary items can be designated as hedged items for foreign exchange risk in consolidated financial statements.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs explain the recognition of exchange differences on monetary items and the exception for net investment items, which informs the nature of the FX exposure.
These paragraphs describe the mechanics of cash flow and fair value hedges, supporting the implementation of hedge accounting for the FX component.

peripheral_authority.reason: This interpretation addresses hedges of net investments in foreign operations, which is a distinct type of hedge and not directly applicable to intragroup dividend receivables.

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting for risk component of intragroup monetary item (exception under IFRS 9 6.3.6)
Ineligibility for hedge accounting (general rule under IFRS 9 6.3.5)

approaches.label: Risk component hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 allows designation of a risk component (e.g., FX risk) of a financial asset as a hedged item, and intragroup monetary items may qualify under the exception in paragraph 6.3.6 if FX differences are not eliminated on consolidation.

approaches.label: No hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Under the general rule in IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5, intragroup items cannot be designated as hedged items in consolidated financial statements unless the exception applies, thus hedge accounting may be disallowed.
